panels.title: Cache Hit Rate
panels.description: Shows the percentage of key lookups that returned a cached value across all Redis instances. A declining hit rate may indicate key evictions or a shift in access patterns.

panels.title: Cache Hits
panels.description: Shows the total number of successful cache lookups in the selected time range. Use alongside cache misses to assess overall cache effectiveness.

panels.title: Cache Misses
panels.description: Shows the total number of cache lookups that did not find a key. A spike in misses can increase load on downstream databases.

panels.title: Replication Offset
panels.description: Shows the current replication offset between primary and replica Redis instances. A growing offset indicates replicas are falling behind the primary.

panels.title: Connected Clients
panels.description: Tracks the number of client connections to Redis over time. A sudden increase may indicate connection leaks; a drop may signal connectivity problems.

panels.title: Memory Used
panels.description: Tracks total memory consumed by Redis over time. Watch for steady growth approaching the maxmemory limit, which triggers evictions.

panels.title: Memory Fragmentation Ratio
panels.description: Tracks the ratio of OS-allocated memory to Redis-used memory over time. Values significantly above 1.0 indicate memory fragmentation; values below 1.0 suggest swapping.

panels.title: Commands Processed
panels.description: Tracks the total Redis command throughput over time. Correlate command volume with latency to distinguish load-driven slowdowns from infrastructure issues.

panels.title: Key Evictions
panels.description: Tracks the number of keys evicted due to memory pressure over time. Non-zero evictions indicate Redis has reached its maxmemory limit and is discarding keys.

panels.title: Redis Instances
panels.description: Lists all detected Redis instances with summary metrics for each. Drill down into an instance to see its detailed performance and memory breakdown.

panels.title: Keyspace Size by DB
panels.description: Shows the number of keys stored in each Redis logical database. Helps identify which databases consume the most memory and drive eviction pressure.

panels.title: Key Expiries by DB
panels.description: Shows the number of expired keys per Redis logical database. A low expiry count relative to keyspace size may mean TTLs are not set, leading to unbounded memory growth.
